outline.text: Portable Document Format (PDF)
Basic Testing Guide
Some tests in this guide require running the built-in Accessibility Check . For quick access to the
accessibility tools used in this guide, add a shortcut to the Accessibility group in the Tools/Task pane.
To create the shortcut, select the Tools tab > Protect & Standardize group > Accessibility > Add . The
Preconditions
A. Is the PDF a PDF Portfolio or does the PDF have file attachments
How to test A
PDF Portfolio
Instruction A.1: Open each individual document/file included in the portfolio and test with the
Test A.1: Was each file tested with the corresponding test process, and did the file pass? If not, the PDF
PDF Attachments
Instruction A.2: Open the PDF and activate the View menu > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes
Attachments . Select and open each attachment separately and test with the corresponding test
Test A.2: Were all attachments tested with the corresponding test process, and did they pass? If not
B. Was the PDF generated from Adobe Designer or LiveCycle Designer
How to test B
Instruction B: Open the File menu > Properties > Document Properties and select the Description
tab (or Ctrl+D ).
Test B: Do the properties for Application: and PDF Producer: display Adobe LiveCycle Designer or
Designer ? If so, the form cannot be tested using Acrobat Pro.
C. Does the PDF contain programming scripts
be tested using software testing methods. The Department of Homeland Security’s (DHS) Section 508
How to test C
Instruction C: Activate the Accessibility Checker ( Tools > Accessibility > Add > Accessibility Check
test the PDF with the DHS’s Section 508 Trusted Tester Conformance Test Process .
D. Is the PDF tagged
How to test D
Instruction D: Activate the File menu > Properties > Description tab to open the Document
Properties window.
Test D: Does Tagged PDF display Yes under the Advanced section in the Document Properties
Description tab? If not, the PDF fails this test.
How to test E
Note: While testing with Acrobat, you must uncheck the options under Edit > Preferences
Instruction E.2: Activate the Accessibility Check ( Tools > Accessibility > Accessibility Check > set the
category to Documents > Start Checking ). Expand the Document testing results.
Test E.2: Does the Accessibility Checker flag Image-only PDF – Failed as an issue? If yes, the PDF fails
Accessibility Check ( Tools > Accessibility Check > Documents > Start Checking ). Expand the
Document category in the Accessibility Checker pane to display issues.
Test E.3: Does Tagged PDF – Failed display under the Document category? If yes, the PDF fails this
F. Does the PDF include tracked changes or have added notes/comments
the Save as PDF feature. All tracked changes are accepted as final and comments are converted to
Exporting to a PDF/XPS file retains the visual appearance of the tracked changes. All text is converted
unintelligible. Notes/comments are tagged as Annotation , strikethrough, underlines for insertions
How to test F
Instruction F.1: To check for untagged annotations (notes/comments) in a tracked changes PDF
activate the Accessibility Check ( Tools > Accessibility Check > Documents > Start Checking ).
Instruction F.2: A PDF that has visual track changes originating from a source document (PDF/XPS
Test F.2 : Does the PDF have visible tracked changes, and is the tagged content inarticulate? If yes, the
Instruction F.3: PDF text markups must have comments explaining the action and content change. To
check markup comments, have accurate descriptions for the action and content change go to Tools
menu> Share & Review section > Comment > Open to access the Comments pane. Verify the
Test F.3: Do all comments associated with the track changes reflect the action and content change? If
Document Properties
1. Does the PDF display a descriptive title when opened
How to test 1
Instruction 1.1: Go to the Description tab in the Document Properties window ( File > Properties
Description ).
Test 1.1: Does the Title field have a descriptive title identifying the document? If not, the PDF fails this
Instruction 1.2: Accessibility Check will automatically fail Document > Title if the initial view is set to
display File Name . Go to File > Properties > Initial View tab and verify Window Options is set to
Show: Document Title.
Test 1.2: Does the Initial View tab display Document Title in the Window Options > Show : field? If
2. Is Assistive Technology access enabled
How to test 2
However, these settings may also block access to assistive technology users, unless the Content
copying for Accessibility option in the Document Properties > Security tab is enabled.
Instruction 2 : Go to File > Properties to open the Document Properties > Security tab and verify the
Content Copying for Accessibility option is set to Allowed .
Test 2: Does Content Copying for Accessibility display Allowed ? If not, the PDF fails this test.
3. Is the appropriate language assigned
How to test 3
Document Language
Document Properties > Advanced tab > Language option.
Note: If the language is not available from the drop-down menu, you can type it in the language field
Instruction 3.1: Go to File > Properties > Document Properties > Advanced tab > Reading Options
Language and verify the PDF language is set to the corresponding document language.
Test 3.1: Does the Language field under the Document Properties > Advanced tab display the
Sections that contain text in languages other than the default document language
Instruction 3.2: Identify any sections in the document where the content text differs from the default
Select the text in the language that differs from the document language. Open the Accessibility Tags
pane ( View > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes > Accessibility Tags ). Find and select the corresponding
tag ( Accessibility tags pane > Options menu > Find Tag from Selection ). With the tag selected, open
the Object Properties window ( Alt + Enter > Content tab > Language ).
Test 3.2: Does the Language field display the selection’s language or a letter code for the
Accessibility Tags
4. Do the accessibility tags follow the visual/logical reading order of the
document
How to test 4
Note: This is a manual check that requires the use of the Accessibility tags pane and the Reading
Order pane or the use of assistive technology to read through the document content.
Instruction 4: Open the Accessibility tags pane ( View menu > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes
Accessibility tags ). Expand the Accessibility tags Options menu and select the Highlight Content
option. Reopen the Accessibility tags Options menu and select the Reading Order option or type x to
Tags tree to show all tags. Use the keyboard’s down arrow key to navigate through the tags. Verify all
in the Tags tree corresponds to the highlighted numbered reading order in the document content.
Test 4: Do all accessibility tags correspond to the numbered reading order of the document content? If
5. Is all decorative content marked as an artifact
How to test 5
Instruction 5: Open the Accessibility tags pane ( View menu > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes
Accessibility tags ). Expand the Accessibility tags Options menu and select Find… to open the Find
button. Select Find > Find Next to highlight and move through all artifacted elements in the
Test: Is all ornamental, trivial, or redundant content and objects marked/identified as artifacts? If not
6. Is vital information in headers, footers, and watermarks tagged
How to test 6
section.
Note: this test is not necessary, if the content in the headers and footers is redundant, repeated, or
Instruction 6: Locate vital information at the beginning of the document or a section, at the top or
Show/Hide > Navigation Panes > Accessibility tags ). Expand the Accessibility tags Options menu
and select the Highlight Content option. Highlight the vital information and expand the Accessibility
tags Options menu and select Find Tag from Selection.
Test 6: Is the vital information in the header, footer and/or watermark tagged, and placed at the
beginning of the tag tree, or the related section? If not, the PDF fails this test.
7. Are all headings in the document tagged with heading tags
How to test 7
Note 1: All headings in source documents must be programmatically identified with an assigned
Instruction 7.1: Open the Accessibility tags pane ( View menu > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes
Accessibility tags ). In the Accessibility tags pane, expand the Tags tree to show all tags. Check the
Accessibility tags Options menu and select Find Tag from Selection . The selected tag should have a
Test 7.1: Are all visually apparent headings in the document tagged with a corresponding H1 through
Instruction 7.2: Locate any nonstandard PDF heading tags (e.g. <Title>, <H_1>, <Section
<Heading3>). Check the appropriate mapping, by opening the Role Map ( Accessibility tags > Options
menu > Edit Role Map ). In the Role Map window expand Document Roles to check mappings
Test 7.2: Are all non-standard heading tags mapped to a standard heading tag? If not, the PDF fails the
Instruction 7.3: Open the Accessibility tags pane ( View menu > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes
Accessibility tags ). Check all heading tags in the document are in logical sequence without skipping a
Note: You could also run the Accessibility Check ( Tools > Accessibility > Open > Accessibility Check
> Start Checking ). The Headings section will display: Appropriate nesting – Failed if headings are
Test 7.3: Are all the headings in the document nested in a logical sequence (<H1>, <H2>, <H3>; <H1
8. Are lists tagged correctly
How to test 8
List (L) tag, with a nested child List Item (LI) tag. All LI tags must have a nested child Label (Lbl) tag
and a child List Item Body (LBody) tag. Multi-level lists must be nested in the last LBody tag of the
Note: Lists originating in Microsoft Word will not export a Label (Lbl) tag. The List Item Body (LBody)
tag includes the bullet or number pertaining to that List Item .
Instruction 8.1: Open the Accessibility tags pane ( View > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes
its corresponding tag ( Accessibility tags Options menu > Find Tag from Selection ). Expand the list
Test 8.1: Does the highlighted content have a parent <L> tag, with nested child List Item (LI) tag(s)
Instruction 8.2: Open the Accessibility tags pane (View > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes
content and find its corresponding tag ( Accessibility tags Options menu > Find Tag from Selection ).
Test 8.2: Are the tiered multilevel lists nested in the last LBody tag of the preceding level? If not, PDF
Objects
9. Do all meaningful images and objects in the PDF have a description of
their purpose or function
How to test 9
contextual description in the form of a caption, nearby text, or Alternate Text is required.
Note 1: Images of, or that contain text (i.e. logos, agency seals) should have the text that is part of the
Note2: Complex Images, charts, graphs, etc. may require a longer description. These types of
Instruction 9: Activate the Accessibility Check ( Tools > Accessibility Check > Documents > Start
Checking ). Expand the Alternate text category in the Accessibility Checker pane to display issues.
Test 9: Does the Alternate text group display Passed results for all listed categories? If not, the PDF
10. Does the PDF have data tables
How to test 10
Data tables in PDFs must be tagged as < Table> . Table rows must be tagged as Table Row <TR>
column and row header cells must be tagged as Table Header Cell (TH) , and data cells must be
tagged as Table Data Cell (TD) . Tables containing multiple header rows, or cells that span multiple
Note: the table editor is a visual tool that is not keyboard accessible. To verify/test the correct reading
and/arrow through the table tags. Assistive technology users may skip the tests in Section 10.
Instruction 10.1: Locate programmatically identified data tables. Open the Accessibility tags pane
( View > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes > Accessibility tags ) and select the first table in the tag tree.
Expand the Accessibility tags Options menu and type the letter q to open the table editor and verify
Test 10.1: Are the table header cells tagged as <TH> and the data cells as <TD>? If not, the PDF fails
Complex tables (tables with multiple header rows/columns, split or merged cells) require Header Cell
ID s to create associations with related header/data cells providing assistive technology users accurate
Instruction 10.2: Open the Accessibility tags pane ( View > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes
Accessibility tags ) and select the first table in the tag tree. Expand the Accessibility tags Options
menu and type the letter q to open the table editor. Select a <TH> cell in the document content
window, right click to open the table editor options, and select Table Cell Properties…(P) . For each
1. Header Cell radio button is checked
2. Scope indicates Column or Row
4. ID: field has a unique identifier
5. If applicable, Associated Header Cell ID s are listed
Note: If the cell tags identifiers ( TH , TD ) do not display, check the Show table cells options in the
Reading Order Pane, or under the Table Editor Options… section in the table editor options pop-up
Test 10.2: Does the header cell scope, row, and column span match the visual layout of the table, and
Instruction 10.3: Complex tables require an additional test to verify table data cells and table header
Open the Accessibility tags pane ( View > Show/Hide > Navigation Panes > Accessibility tags ) and
select the first table in the tag tree. Expand the Accessibility tags Options menu and type the letter q
to open the table editor. Select a <TD> cell in the document content window, right click to open the
table editor options, and select Table Cell Properties…(P) . For each data cell verify
1. Data Cell radio button is checked
2. Attributes field for Row Span and Column Span are correct
3. Associated Header Cell IDs: for row and column headers are listed
Test D: Are Data Cells associated with Header Cell IDs (Figure 29)? If not, the PDF fails this test.
11. Does the PDF have links and controls
How to test 11
Instruction 11 : Press the Tab key to navigate to links and user controls in the content. Check that each
identified link has an unambiguous name that describes its destination, function, and/or purpose OR
Test 11: Does the link have a unique name that describes the destination, function, and/or purpose
OR that is determinable within context? If not, the PDF fails this test.
12. Is the PDF a fillable form
How to test 12
Instruction 12.1: All form fields must have a tooltip, and the tool tip must match its label or
Test 12.1: When you tab through or hover over each form field, is a tooltip revealed and does the
Instruction 12.2: Press the tab key to find form fields (text fields, radio buttons, checkboxes, combo
Test 12.2: When you tab through the PDF, does the tab order match the visually logical order of the
13. Does meaningful information conveyed only with color or other
visual characteristics also have a text description
How to test 13
Instruction 13: Find where color and/or other visual characteristics, such as green square in right
Test 13: Is there text that conveys the meaning of the color or other visual characteristics? If not, the
14. Is the contrast ratio between text and background sufficient
How to test 14
Instruction 14: Open the Colour Contrast Analyser application. Drag the Foreground eyedropper icon
Test 14: Does color contrast ratio of Foreground (text) and Background ratio meet the minimum
Note: To check for color blindness simulation with any preview in the testing window, open the View
menu and select the Colour Blindness simulation ( Ctrl+B ) option.
Miscellaneous
15. Does the PDF contain audio-only, video-only, or multimedia objects
that contain meaningful information
How to test 15
Note: If the PDF does not contain audio, video, or multimedia files, you do not need to perform this
Instruction 15: Activate the meaningful audio-only object
Test 15.1: Does the audio-only object have transcript that is accurate and complete? If not, the PDF
Test 15.2: Does the video-only object have text description that is accurate and complete? If not, the
Test 15.3: Does the multimedia (audio and video) have synchronized captions and audio description
16. Does the PDF have flashing objects
How to test 16
17. Does the PDF need an accessible alternate version
How to test 17
